title: Supported Media Types
html:
Supported Media Types

Supported Media Types

HTTP requests commonly include a Content-Type header that describes the type and format of the data in the message body. The value of the Content-Type is the media type, commonly known as the MIME type.

Uniface supports a limited number of MIME types. This means that it can interpret, process, and display the data without any other intervention.

If a Uniface static server page or the UHTTP component receives a request or response with of a non-supported MIME type, such as a video or Word document, it can save the content to a file or load it into an HTTP request for transport, but it can do nothing else with it.

Table 1. MIME Types Supported by Uniface

MIME Type

Description

application/x-www-form-urlencoded

Used with GET and POST methods to provide input to HTML forms.

multipart/form-data

Used with POST methods to provide input to HTML forms, when the data input is too large for a query string. Used for binary data.
Note: The UHTTP file operations do not support multipart/form-data messages.

text/

Used with all types of HTTP requests.

text/xml

Used with PUT, POST, and PATCH methods that contain XML data, such as Uniface entity parameters.

application/json

Used with PUT, POST, and PATCH methods that contain JSON data, which is often used in RESTful web services.

Static Server Pages

When Uniface receives an HTTP request for a static server page (USP), it puts the headers into the HTTPREQUESTHEADERS channel of $webinfo and the body of the message into the INPUT channel. The HTTP method specified in the HTTP request determines the way the data in the INPUT channel is formatted. For more information, see $webinfo.

For GET and DELETE requests, the message body consists of the query string defined in the URL, so there is no need to specify a content type. By default, it is treated as text/.

For PUT, POST, and PATCH requests, the MIME type must be specified.

For content type: application/json, USPs support all UTF-8, UTF-16 and UTF-32 character set encodings for HTTP requests, but use only UTF-8 encoding in the HTTP response.

UHTTP

The UHTTP component can be used to communicate with any HTTP server. The operations it provides can be activated by any Uniface component, making it possible to address web services and other sites for example to upload and download files.

For content type: application/json, UHTTP only supports the default UTF-8 character set, and the charset attribute should not be specified. If It is specified, it will ignore any value other than UTF-8.

Although some REST APIs offer multi-part messages, these are not supported by the UHTTP file operations.

LikeDislike
